parameters.content:
## WhatsApp MCP Server, built in n8n

Turns this n8n workflow into an **MCP server** so any MCP client — **Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline** — can control WhatsApp through SocialMate as native tools. It also **sidesteps the community-node tool bug** (n8n #26202), because MCP tools run outside n8n's internal tool queue.

**Setup**
1. Set **`N8N_COMMUNITY_PACKAGES_ALLOW_TOOL_USAGE=true`** on the n8n instance.
2. One **SocialMate API** credential on each SocialMate tool node (admin-scope key; `http://127.0.0.1:3456` or your Pro named tunnel). *Read/Get AI Context are Pro.*
3. **Activate**. Open the **MCP Server Trigger** node and copy its **Production SSE URL**.
4. In Claude Desktop's MCP config, add the server:
   `npx mcp-remote <SSE_URL> --header "Authorization: Bearer <your-n8n-auth>"`.

Each tool's parameters use **`$fromAI(...)`**, so the MCP client's model fills in the recipient, text, etc. Add more SocialMate tool nodes (Send Media, Queue, Group…) to widen what the agent can do.

> Prefer a **standalone** server that doesn't need n8n? SocialMate also ships a native MCP server — see the app's **API & Integrations → MCP** panel and `docs/AI-AGENT-TOOL-GUIDE.md`.
